text: We present NeuroVault (NeuroVault.org), an open-source, web-based repository designed for the storage, sharing, and interactive visualization of unthresholded statistical maps of the human brain. While traditional neuroimaging publications typically report only peak coordinates—a practice that obscures the full spatial extent of activations and limits meta-analytic power—NeuroVault allows researchers to upload full 3D volumes. The platform features integrated web-based viewers for exploring data in both volumetric and surface spaces and leverages the Neurosynth database to provide real-time cognitive decoding. By exposing data through a public REST API and utilizing CC0 licensing, NeuroVault promotes interoperability with other neuroinformatics tools and facilitates image-based meta-analyses (IBMA). To demonstrate the utility of the repository, we present a proof-of-concept meta-analysis of response inhibition, showing that a small number of unthresholded maps can recover activation and deactivation patterns captured by much larger coordinate-based databases. NeuroVault addresses the critical need for long-term preservation of machine-readable results, providing a centralized resource to enhance the transparency and reproducibility of brain mapping research.
